ddls:
CREATE TABLE "operations" (
  "operation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each operation */,
  "operation_name" TEXT /* Name of the operation */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the operation */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the operation */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the operation */,
  "duration_months" INTEGER /* Duration of the operation in months */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the operation */,
  "mission_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the mission type associated with the operation */,
  "commander_name" TEXT /* Name of the commander overseeing the operation */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the operation (e.g., planned, ongoing, completed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("operation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_operations_mission_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("mission_type_id") REFERENCES mission_types ("mission_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "costs" (
  "cost_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cost entry */,
  "operation_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the operation associated with the cost */,
  "additional_operating_cost" REAL /* Additional operating costs for the operation in millions */,
  "additional_capital_investment" REAL /* Additional capital investment for the operation in millions */,
  "year" TEXT /* Fiscal year for the cost */,
  "cost_type" TEXT /* Type of cost (e.g., personnel, equipment, logistics) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cost_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_costs_operation_id FOREIGN KEY ("operation_id") REFERENCES operations ("operation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "personnel" (
  "personnel_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each personnel record */,
  "operation_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the operation associated with the personnel record */,
  "total_personnel" INTEGER /* Total number of personnel involved in the operation */,
  "troop_type" TEXT /* Type of troop engaged in the operation (e.g., combat, support) */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of personnel deployment */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of personnel withdrawal */,
  "casualties" INTEGER /* Number of casualties during the operation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("personnel_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_personnel_operation_id FOREIGN KEY ("operation_id") REFERENCES operations ("operation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "mission_types" (
  "mission_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mission type */,
  "mission_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the mission type (e.g., peacekeeping, combat) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the mission type */,
  "min_personnel_required" INTEGER /* Minimum number of personnel required for the mission type */,
  "max_duration" INTEGER /* Maximum duration for the mission type in months */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mission_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "logistics" (
  "logistics_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each logistics entry */,
  "operation_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the operation associated with the logistics entry */,
  "equipment_used" TEXT /* Equipment used during the operation */,
  "transport_means" TEXT /* Means of transport used in the operation */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of logistical support */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of logistical support */,
  "suppliers" TEXT /* Suppliers of the equipment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("logistics_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_logistics_operation_id FOREIGN KEY ("operation_id") REFERENCES operations ("operation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., planner, analyst, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the user (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "operation_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operation being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the operation data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the operation data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access occurred */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device used for access (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_operation_id FOREIGN KEY ("operation_id") REFERENCES operations ("operation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
